name: Anginares Me Anitho - Artichokes with Dill
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Chopped scallions, white onion only
1/4 cup Olive oil or other oil
recipeInstructions:
Serves: 4 as a light meal, 8 as a first course
Cooking time: 45-50 minutes
1. Wash artichokes well and cut off stems close to the base.
2. Prepare a bowl of cold water with lemon juice and lemon slices.
3. If desired, stir in 2-3 tablespoons flour to prevent discoloration.
4. Rub each prepared artichoke cut surface with a lemon slice from the bowl and place them in it until all are ready.
5. Cook as soon as possible after preparation.
6. Remove 3 or 4 layers of leaves until tender inner leaves remain. Scoop out choke and pink thorny leaves from the center using a spoon or melon ball scoop. Cut in half.
7. In a large pan, gently fry spring onions in oil until soft.
8. Add lemon juice, water, about 2 teaspoons salt, and freshly ground white pepper. Bring to a boil.
9. Drain prepared artichokes and add them to the pan with dill. Return to a slow simmer, cover, and cook gently for 30 minutes or until artichokes are tender.
10. Reduce cooking liquid in a separate pan until reduced to half its original quantity (about 1-1/2 cups). Keep artichokes hot in a warm oven.
11. Mix cornflour with cold water to form a paste, then stir into the simmering liquid and cook until thickened and bubbling.
12. Beat eggs in a mixing bowl until light and frothy. Gradually add remaining lemon juice. Gradually pour in the simmering stock while beating constantly.
13. Return mixture to the pan and stir over low heat for 1-2 minutes to cook the egg.
14. Pile artichokes on a warm platter, pour sauce over top, and sprinkle with chopped dill. Serve as a light meal or first course.
